'''Zaloriis City''' was the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zaloriis/Legends|Zaloriis]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] constructed a base there, but it was destroyed by forces led by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]].

==History==
Zaloriis [[City/Legends|City]] was the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] city of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Zaloriis/Legends|Zaloriis]], a planet belonging to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] built a base there and [[Zalor Minister|Minister]] [[Transe Decar]] planned on seceding from the Galactic Empire. Zaloriis stopped sending its [[Standard month/Legends|monthly]] tribute to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] visited to see why. He was attacked by [[Zalorian Separatists]] led by Minister Decar, but overwhelmed them and rescued [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Maximilian Veers/Legends|Maximilian Veers]]. Veers led him to [[Camp Culroon]], a research base where he outfitted ''[[Blizzard 1/Legends|Blizzard 1]]'', a prototype [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]]. Veers then [[Zaloriis campaign/Legends|assaulted]] Zaloriis City and destroyed the base there. At the time of its destruction, Zaloriis City featured two [[Fortress/Legends|Fortresses]] and a number of other military buildings, including a [[Command center]]. The city was surrounded by a wall and was protected by [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser turrets]].

After the battle had been won, Veers tapped found out the approximate [[Hoth/Legends|new location]] of the Rebel Alliance in the [[Elrood sector/Legends|Elrood sector]].

==Behind the scenes==
Zaloriis City appears in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'' during the Darth Vader campaign. The campaign is also included in the game's [[2002]] expansion pack, ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Clone Campaigns]]''.
